channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Watch: Potential albino pygmy killer whale spotted off Hawaii
item.description: Whale watchers around Hawaii are crossing their fingers for another glimpse of what could be an albino whale.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, KRON4

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read's retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Jury selection began Tuesday in the retrial of Karen Read, less than a year after a judge declared a mistrial on charges that she was responsible for the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, KRON4

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Cast for Beatles biopics revealed: Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, Joseph Quinn and Harris Dickinson to star
item.description: Sony Pictures kicked off the annual CinemaCon convention and trade show in Las Vegas on Monday by touting their four upcoming biopics told from the perspective of each member of The Beatles.
item.dc:creator: Krysta Fauria, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cornell student protester facing deportation leaves the US on his 'own terms' after losing faith
item.description: A Cornell University student facing deportation after his visa was revoked over campus activism said he decided to leave the United States.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Almost 40 percent of Americans report facing extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Nearly 40 percent of Americans experienced extreme weather last year — and those who did were more likely to be concerned about climate change, according to new polling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Miami's Haitian community gathers in prayer as crises escalate in homeland and US
item.description: Packed pews, rollicking singing and emotional devotions have marked Lent worship services at Notre Dame d'Haiti, the Catholic church at the heart of the largest Haitian diaspora in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photos show living conditions of Connecticut man allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: Dozens of photos obtained by Nexstar's WTNH show the living conditions authorities found in the Connecticut home where a man says he was held captive by his stepmom for over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: Former Colombian Navy lieutenant sentenced to 15 years in drug case
item.description: The former Navy lieutenant recruited active members to put GPS devices on Colombian Navy boats to help drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas judge jails man in Tesla firebombing case pending trial
item.description: A federal judge ordered the man accused of firebombing a Tesla collision center jailed pending trial.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Sewage concerns arise as county plans to reopen San Diego campground
item.description: Though the campground offers stunning views of the valley, enjoying all that it has to offer could be risky due to the ongoing sewage problem.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, NewsNation

item.title: UPDATE: Dothan students injured after suspected tornado hits school
item.description: DOTHAN, Ala (WDHN) -- A suspected tornado hit the downtown area of Dothan, Alabama, Monday afternoon, damaging a portion of Dothan Preparatory Academy. Dothan City School officials confirmed to WDHN that 5 students sustained minor and moderate injuries when the severe weather hit the school.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. About half of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Driver facing multiple charges in Brooklyn crash that killed mother, two children
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman is facing multiple charges after she crashed into a family out for a walk in Brooklyn, killing a mother and her two children in what New York City Mayor Eric Adams described as a “tragic accident of a Shakespearean proportion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Protesters against Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government demonstrated outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and Europe on Saturday in the latest attempt to dent the fortune of the world’s richest man.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oliver Stone to testify at House hearing on JFK files
item.description: American filmmaker Oliver Stone — whose political thriller, “JFK,” made waves more than 30 years ago — will testify on Tuesday before a House committee on the release of new documents related to the assassination of John F. Kennedy Jr., according to a press release from the committee.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces crucial week on the economy
item.description: President Trump is entering a critical week for the economy amid growing fears that his penchant for tariffs could stall growth and undercut progress on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: 3 US soldiers found dead in armored vehicle in Lithuania; 1 still missing
item.description: Three of the U.S. Army soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have been found dead in their armored vehicle that was pulled from a swampy area early Monday, according to U.S. Army Europe and Africa Command. Another soldier is still missing.
item.dc:creator: By LIUDAS DAPKUS and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: North Carolina woman allegedly murdered Green Beret husband, concealed his death: officials
item.description: North Carolina authorities have arrested retired Green Beret Clinton Bonnell's wife, Shana Cloud, in connection with his January disappearance and murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Explosion at Detroit apartment building injures residents, including several children
item.description: When firefighters arrived, some people were in windows of the building “kind of hanging off ready to jump,” Fire Commissioner Chuck Simms told reporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their disagreements often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tsunami warning lifted after 7.0 earthquake near Tonga in the South Pacific
item.description: A strong 7.0 magnitude earthquake hit near Tonga, prompting an initial tsunami warning that was later lifted for the Pacific island country
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, ABC 4

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: See a missing police officer post? It's likely part of a nationwide scam, authorities say
item.description: Have you seen a post on Facebook recently, asking you to be on the lookout for a missing police officer? Chances are, you're actually seeing a scam post, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KRON4

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Statham’s 'A Working Man’ upsets ‘Snow White’ to take No. 1 at the box office
item.description: In an unexpected upset, the Jason Statham thriller “A Working Man” took No. 1 at the box office, besting the rapidly declining “Snow White,” according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WGN 9
